era_context: Continuation of the Middle Bronze Age in the Levant and Egypt; the Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; Hittite Old Kingdom in Anatolia; Indus Valley Civilization in decline; traditional Xia or early Shang in China (unconfirmed by archaeology).
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for this year in any surviving source tradition. The year falls within the reign of Samsu-iluna in Babylon, but no year-specific documentation exists.
historiographic_note: This year exemplifies the severe documentation gap for the ancient world. Even in Mesopotamia, where cuneiform records are relatively abundant, administrative texts do not provide year-by-year narrative histories. Chronological systems are reconstructed from king lists, astronomical calculations, and radiocarbon dating, all of which carry uncertainty. For most of the world in 1772 BCE, no written records exist at all.
